summary: First-finish smoke test for Flask Expense Tracker (server-rendered, all routes under /api). Backend health, CSRF, auth guard, demo login, dashboard stats, transactions CRUD, filters, reports charts, budget save, dark mode, logout and CSV export all verified end-to-end via curl + Playwright against the public preview URL.
passed: GET /api/login renders form with CSRF token
Login with demo@demo.com / demo123 succeeds and redirects to /api/dashboard
Invalid credentials show 'Invalid credentials.' flash message
Dashboard stat cards match spec exactly: income=$3000.00, expense=$540.00, balance=$2460.00, count=8
Dashboard budget block shows Budget=$1500 / Spent=$540 / Remaining=$960 with progress bar
Transactions page renders 8 rows (tx-row-* testids) with all columns
Search filter 'Pharmacy' narrows to 1 matching row
Clear filters resets back to 8 rows
Add transaction (amount=42.50, category=Food, type=expense) creates new row visible on list
Reports page: monthlyChart and categoryChart canvases both rendered (width>0)
Budget page: setting amount=1500 saves and progress bar renders
Theme toggle flips data-theme light->dark and persists in localStorage
Logout redirects to /api/login and subsequent /api/dashboard requires login
CSV export returns text/csv with header 'Date,Type,Category,Description,Amount'
action_items: Optional: populate /app/memory/test_credentials.md with the demo user (demo@demo.com / demo123) so future testers don't have to rely on the review request.
notes: Did not exhaustively test: signup, edit/delete transaction, category/type dropdown filters, profile update + password change re-login, pagination beyond 10 rows, and mobile responsive breakpoint. Core happy path is solid; these can be covered by full testing agent post first-finish.
